title: [FIRRTL] Fix use of layer-enabled rwprobe's with force, force_initial
body:
```firrtl
FIRRTL version 4.0.0

circuit L:
  layer T, bind:

  public module L:
    output p : RWProbe<UInt<1>, T>

    layerblock T:
      wire w : UInt<1>
      connect w, UInt<1>(0)
      define p = rwprobe(w)
      force_initial(p, UInt<1>(1))
```

Fails while verifying constraint on `force_initial` which expects the destination type to be something that can be inferred from the source (rwprobe of that type):

```
layers-rwprobe-5.fir:13:7: error: 'firrtl.ref.force_initial' op failed to verify that reference type of dest should be RWProbe of src
      force_initial(p, UInt<1>(1))
      ^
layers-rwprobe-5.fir:13:7: note: see current operation: "firrtl.ref.force_initial"(%2, %arg0, %1) : (!firrtl.uint<1>, !firrtl.rwprobe<uint<1>, @T>, !firrtl.const.uint<1>) -> ()
```

I believe the introduction of colors means that these operations no longer can infer/omit the type of their rwprobe argument, since it's unknown the layers that may be present.
